reason: Risk of thermal runaway and fire; batteries are Class 9 Miscellaneous Dangerous Goods under IMDG and IATA regulations.
responsible_department: IMO (sea) / IATA (air) / national civil aviation and maritime authorities
required_certificates: Dangerous Goods Declaration (DGD) if above threshold watt-hours
notes: Damaged or defective batteries cannot be shipped without prior approval from the National Competent Authority. Banned on passenger aircraft as cargo since 2016.

product: Lithium-ion batteries (packed with or in equipment)
reason: Same fire/thermal runaway risk as standalone cells, packaged with or inside equipment (e.g. laptops, power tools).
notes: Packing Instruction PI966 (packed with equipment) or PI967 (in equipment) applies.

reason: Explosion and fire hazard; classified under Class 1 Explosives with strict segregation and stowage rules.
responsible_department: National explosives regulatory authority (e.g. PESO in India, ATF in the USA)
notes: Subject to strict import/export licensing in most countries; often requires prior government authorization.

reason: Contents under pressure; risk of rupture, fire, or explosion if mishandled.
notes: Limited quantity exemptions may apply for small consumer packagings.

reason: Highly corrosive; causes severe burns and can damage transport equipment on leakage.
notes: Requires corrosion-resistant packaging and segregation from incompatible materials during transport.

reason: Emits ionizing radiation; strictly regulated due to health risks from radiation exposure.
responsible_department: National nuclear/radiological regulatory authority (e.g. AERB in India, NRC in the USA)
notes: Requires specialized packaging and handling; often needs prior regulatory notification before shipment.

reason: Toxic to humans and the environment if ingested, inhaled, or contacted.
notes: Many pesticides are additionally restricted or banned under the Rotterdam Convention (Prior Informed Consent).

reason: Explosive projection hazard; export/import of ammunition is prohibited or requires special government authorization in most jurisdictions.
responsible_department: National defense/arms control authority (e.g. DDGFT in India, State Department in the USA)
notes: Generally prohibited for standard commercial shipping; requires government-to-government authorization.

reason: Known carcinogen; banned for import/export in most countries under the Rotterdam Convention.
notes: Banned entirely in the EU and many other jurisdictions; some countries allow limited exceptions with strict controls.

reason: Standard consumer goods with no inherent transport hazard.
notes: Subject to standard customs and HS code classification only; refer to the Trader Agent for duty and HS code guidance.

reason: No inherent transport hazard when shipped without embedded lithium batteries.
notes: If the product contains a battery, check the lithium battery entries instead.
